folio.label: 169v
folio.l:
they shuln come or what shal finaly bitide of hem in this cas
ne can I nat deme , but by co~iectynge , & by supposynge ,

For
we shuln suppose , æ æei shuln come to a wykked ende ,

by cause æ
æe book of Decrees seitú ¢ Selden or w gret peyne , ben causes I
brougút to good ende , whan æei ben badly bigo~ne

¢ Now sire if
men wolde axen me , why æ god suffred men , to do ©ow æis vile-
nye ; Certes I can nat wel answere , as for no sotúfastnesse ,

for
the Apostle seitú ; æ æe sciences & the Iugementz of oure lord
god almygúty ben ful depe ,

æäe may no man co~pähende ne serche~
he~ suffisau~tly

¢ Nathelees by certeyne päsumpcions & co~iectyngã
I holde , & bileue ,

æ god whicú æ is ful of Iustice & of rigútwysnesse ,
hatú suffred æis bitide , by Iuste cause resonable ,,

Thy name is
Melibe , this is to seyn , a man æ drynketú hony ,

æè hast dronke so
muche hony , of swete temporel richesses & delices & honoures of
this world ,
æ æè art dronken , & hast forgeten Ihu~ cést æi creaturö ,
æè ne hast nat don to hi~ swicú honoå & reuäence , as the ougúte ,
ne æè ne hast nat wel I taken kepe , to the wordes of Ouyde , æat seitú

vnder the hony of the goodes of the body , is hid the venym æ
sleetú the soule ,,

And Salamon seitú ; If æè hast founden hony
ete of it , æ suffisetú ;

for if æè ete of it , out of mesure , æè shalt
spewe & be nedy & poure ,

& ¹auentåe Crist hatú the in despit , &
hatú tåned a wey fro the , his face & hise Eres of misericorde ,

and
also he hatú suffred , æ æè hast ben punysshed , in the manere æ æè
hast I trespasced
¢ Thow hast don sy~ne , ageyn oure lord Crist ,

For certes the thre enemys of mankynde , æ is to seyn ; æe flessú ,
the fend , & the world ,

æè hast suffred he~ , entre , in to thyn herte ,
wilfully , by the wyndowes of thy body ,

& hast nat defended
æi self , suffisau~tly a geyns here assautes , & here temptacions ,
so æ they han wounded thy soule , in fyue places ,

this is to
seyn x double check/ODR/ the dedly sy~nes , æ ben entred in to thyn herte , by thyne
Fyue wyttes ,,

And in æe same manere , oure lord cést hatú wold
& suffred , æ thyne thre enemys , ben entred in to thyn hous , by
the wyndowes ,

& han I wounded thy dougúterö in the forseid ma-
nere

¢ Certes q¶ Melibe , I se wel æ ©e enforce ©ow muchel , by
wordes , to ouäcomen me in swicú a manere , æ I shal nat venge
me of myne enemys ,

shewynge me the ¹ils & the yueles , æat
mygúten falle of æis vengeau~ce ,,
a man wold neuäe take vengeau~ce , & æ were harm ,

for by the vengeau~ce takynge , ben the wykked men , disseuered
fro the goode men ,

And they æ han wil , to do wykkednesse , resträy-
nen here wikked påpos , whan they sen the punysshynge and the
